meta.project: MyProdusen HRIS & Employee Operations Web App

product_overview: MyProdusen is an internal HRIS web application for Produsen Dimsum Medan that digitizes employee operations such as authentication, attendance, leave, KPI, reporting, notifications, and user management. The product is designed for mobile-first use by employees and a full administrative experience for Superadmin, with secure server-side authorization and production-ready PostgreSQL backend services.
core_goals: Provide secure employee and Superadmin authentication with account activation and password recovery flows.
Enable Superadmin to manage users, employee profiles, and role/status assignments with production-safe role exposure only.
Support GPS- and selfie-based attendance check-in/check-out with geofence validation and protected selfie storage.
Allow employees to submit leave requests and support approval/rejection workflows with balance tracking and audit logging.
Deliver KPI template, assignment, scoring, and approval workflows with RBAC enforcement.
Provide role-aware reports, payroll views, overtime, notifications, announcements, health, and version endpoints for operational visibility.
Ensure all sensitive actions are protected by server-side RBAC, audit logs, and persistent storage best practices.

features.name: Authentication API
features.description: Handles public registration, login/logout, profile retrieval, account activation, forgot/reset password, and authenticated password changes for employees and Superadmins.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/public-register with username, email, and password -> Receive 200/201 with inactive employee account created and activation email queued -> POST /api/auth/activate with backend-issued activation token -> Receive 200 with account activated -> POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT cookie and authenticated user payload -> GET /api/auth/profile with auth cookie -> Receive 200 with current user profile
POST /api/auth/login with incorrect password -> Receive 401 with AUTH_INVALID_CREDENTIALS error -> GET /api/auth/profile without auth cookie -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

POST /api/auth/login with credentials for an inactive user -> Receive 403 with AUTH_USER_INACTIVE error -> POST /api/auth/forgot-password with unknown email -> Receive 200 or safe success response without revealing account existence
POST /api/auth/change-password without auth cookie -> Receive 401 Unauthorized -> POST /api/auth/logout without auth cookie -> Receive 401 Unauthorized or auth failure response due to missing session

features.name: Employees And Users API
features.description: Supports Superadmin user listing and role/status updates, employee creation and linkage, employee profile creation, and employee record retrieval and maintenance.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login as Superadmin with valid credentials -> Receive 200 and auth cookie -> GET /api/users -> Receive 200 with production-safe user list -> PATCH /api/users with target user role and active status -> Receive 200 with updated user record -> POST /api/users/[id]/employee-profile for a registered user -> Receive 200 with linked employee profile created

POST /api/employees with employee master data and linked user information -> Receive 200 with created employee and generated NIP -> GET /api/employees with filters and pagination -> Receive 200 with employee list

GET /api/employees/[id] for an employee owned by the authenticated user or permitted Superadmin -> Receive 200 with employee detail -> PUT /api/employees/[id] with updated profile fields -> Receive 200 with saved updates -> DELETE /api/employees/[id] -> Receive 200 with deactivate/delete action handled by service policy

GET /api/employees/[id] without permission or for another employee's record -> Receive 403 with AUTH_FORBIDDEN or RBAC denial -> POST /api/users/[id]/employee-profile for a user that does not exist -> Receive 404 or service not-found error

features.name: Attendance API
features.description: Provides daily attendance state, check-in/check-out operations, exception listing, full attendance listing, and protected access to selfie files with geo-fence and selfie validation.
user_flows: GET /api/attendance/today with valid auth cookie -> Receive 200 with current employee daily attendance state -> POST /api/attendance/check-in with GPS coordinates, accuracy, selfie metadata, and valid shift/location context -> Receive 200 with attendance saved and status determined -> GET /api/attendance/selfie/[...path] for own protected selfie file -> Receive 200 with authorized file access

POST /api/attendance/check-in before shift is active or with missing GPS/selfie data -> Receive 400 with ATTENDANCE_GPS_REQUIRED or ATTENDANCE_SELFIE_REQUIRED error -> POST /api/attendance/check-in outside geofence when rejection is enabled -> Receive 400 with ATTENDANCE_OUTSIDE_GEOFENCE or pending-review outcome based on policy

POST /api/attendance/check-out without a prior check-in -> Receive 400 with ATTENDANCE_NOT_CHECKED_IN error -> POST /api/attendance/check-out after a completed checkout -> Receive 400 with ATTENDANCE_ALREADY_CHECKED_OUT error -> GET /api/attendance/exceptions as Superadmin -> Receive 200 with review queue or exception list

GET /api/attendance without the required RBAC scope -> Receive 403 with authorization failure -> GET /api/attendance/selfie/[...path] for another user's file without ownership or RBAC -> Receive 403 with protected access denied

features.name: Leave API
features.description: Enables employees to submit leave-related requests, allows Superadmin approval or rejection, and exposes leave balance summaries and ledger history.
user_flows: POST /api/leave with leave type, date range, and reason -> Receive 200 with pending leave request created and balance held -> GET /api/leave/balance -> Receive 200 with current annual leave balance summary -> GET /api/leave/balance/history -> Receive 200 with ledger history

POST /api/leave/[id]/approve for a pending request -> Receive 200 with approved status and balance updated -> POST /api/leave/[id]/reject with rejection reason -> Receive 200 with rejected status and audit log created

POST /api/leave with an overlapping active leave request -> Receive 400 with LEAVE_OVERLAP error -> POST /api/leave/[id]/reject without a reason -> Receive 400 with validation error for missing rejection reason

GET /api/leave as a user without RBAC access to the target scope -> Receive 403 with authorization failure -> POST /api/leave/[id]/approve for a request that is not pending -> Receive 400 or service error indicating invalid state transition

features.name: KPI API
features.description: Manages KPI templates, assignments, results, scoring updates, and approval workflows with strict RBAC and template weight validation.
user_flows: GET /api/kpi/templates -> Receive 200 with KPI template list -> POST /api/kpi/templates with valid template items and total weight validation -> Receive 200 with created KPI template -> POST /api/kpi/assignments -> Receive 200 with assignment created -> POST /api/kpi/results with employee KPI score input -> Receive 200 with saved result -> POST /api/kpi/results/[id]/approve -> Receive 200 with approved KPI result

POST /api/kpi/templates with total weight not matching policy -> Receive 400 with KPI_TEMPLATE_INVALID_WEIGHT error -> POST /api/kpi/results/[id]/approve on an already approved result -> Receive 400 with KPI_RESULT_ALREADY_APPROVED error

GET /api/kpi/results without permission to view the target employee -> Receive 403 with RBAC denial -> POST /api/kpi/results with unauthorized attempt to edit another employee's score -> Receive 403 or validation failure

features.name: Reports Payroll Overtime Notifications API
features.description: Provides attendance reporting and PDF export, payroll period and own-payroll access, overtime request/rate listing, notifications, announcements, and public health/version endpoints.
user_flows: GET /api/reports/attendance with filters as Superadmin -> Receive 200 with attendance report data -> GET /api/reports/pdf with the same protected scope -> Receive 200 with generated PDF report -> GET /api/payroll/periods -> Receive 200 with payroll periods -> GET /api/payroll/me as authenticated employee -> Receive 200 with own payroll data

GET /api/overtime with RBAC scope -> Receive 200 with overtime request list -> POST /api/overtime with overtime request payload -> Receive 200 with created overtime request -> GET /api/overtime/rates -> Receive 200 with overtime rate list -> GET /api/notifications -> Receive 200 with current user notifications -> GET /api/announcements -> Receive 200 with announcement list

GET /api/reports/attendance without permission -> Receive 403 with authorization failure -> GET /api/reports/pdf with missing auth cookie -> Receive 401 Unauthorized -> POST /api/overtime with invalid or incomplete payload -> Receive 400 with validation error
GET /api/health without auth -> Receive 200 with public safe health status -> GET /api/version without auth -> Receive 200 with public safe application version metadata

known_limitations.issue: Backend happy-path tests require deterministic local seed credentials and domain fixtures.
known_limitations.impact: Employee attendance, overtime, KPI, payroll, and notification flows may block without seeded data.

known_limitations.issue: Account activation and reset-password happy paths require backend-issued JWT tokens.
known_limitations.impact: Tests using arbitrary tokens should fail by design.

known_limitations.issue: Local TestSprite tunnel requires explicit non-production bypass flags for rate-limit and CSRF origin checks.
known_limitations.impact: Never enable bypass flags in production.
